meta.description: Supply, demand and risk signals shown on The Crude Oracle dashboards. SAMPLE / INDICATIVE placeholder values — update daily. See docs/DAILY_UPDATE_GUIDE.md.

supply.label: OPEC / OPEC+ Position
supply.value: Voluntary cuts extended; unwinding schedule under review
supply.detail: OPEC+ ministers maintained current voluntary production cuts and signalled a data-dependent approach to unwinding. Compliance remains the key watch item.

supply.label: US Crude Inventories
supply.detail: Commercial crude stocks drew against expectations of a small build. Cushing levels remain below the five-year average.

supply.label: US Rig Count
supply.detail: Oil-directed rigs broadly flat week on week. Capital discipline continues to cap US supply growth.

supply.label: Global Spare Capacity
supply.detail: Effective spare capacity is concentrated in a small number of OPEC+ members, keeping the market sensitive to supply disruption.

supply.label: Refinery Utilisation
supply.detail: Seasonally strong runs into the driving season. Watch unplanned outages and turnaround schedules.

supply.label: Tanker / Shipping Risk
supply.detail: A meaningful share of tanker traffic continues to route around the Cape of Good Hope, extending voyage times and supporting freight rates.

supply.label: Key Export Terminal Notes
supply.detail: Major Gulf, US Gulf Coast and North Sea loading programmes operating normally. Weather watch on the US Gulf Coast during hurricane season.

demand.label: China Demand
demand.detail: Crude imports and refinery throughput show tentative recovery. Petrochemical feedstock demand remains the bright spot.

demand.label: India Demand
demand.detail: Diesel and gasoline consumption continue to grow year on year. India remains the largest single source of incremental global oil demand.

demand.label: US Demand
demand.detail: Implied gasoline demand tracking the five-year average through the summer driving season.

demand.label: Aviation Fuel Signal
demand.detail: Jet fuel demand continues its multi-year recovery, led by Asia-Pacific international capacity.

demand.label: Diesel Demand Signal
demand.detail: Middle distillate demand remains the weak link in the barrel, tracking soft manufacturing PMIs in Europe.

demand.label: Refinery Margin Signal
demand.value: Mid-cycle — gasoline cracks firm, diesel soft
demand.detail: Composite refining margins around mid-cycle levels. Gasoline strength offsetting distillate weakness.

risk.label: Geopolitical Risk
risk.detail: Middle East tensions, Russia sanctions enforcement and shipping-lane security remain the principal supply-side risk premia in crude pricing.
risk.source: The Crude Oracle assessment of public reporting

risk.label: OPEC Alert
risk.detail: Market positioning ahead of the next OPEC+ meeting is the key near-term event risk for crude prices.

risk.label: LNG Signal
risk.value: Balanced — new supply vs Asian demand
risk.detail: New liquefaction capacity ramping while Asian spot demand stays price-sensitive. European storage refill pace is the swing factor.

bottom_line.what_moved: Crude extended its grind higher over the past fortnight; Brent holding the upper-$78/low-$79 area after another modest US stock draw.
bottom_line.why_it_moved: A larger-than-expected stock draw, resilient summer demand indicators and persistent shipping disruption offset macro caution.
bottom_line.what_matters_next: OPEC+ signalling on the pace of unwinding voluntary cuts, plus weekly EIA inventory data.
bottom_line.biggest_risk: A faster-than-expected return of OPEC+ barrels into a soft diesel demand environment.
bottom_line.biggest_opportunity: Quality upstream producers with low break-evens remain attractively valued relative to strip prices (watchlist, not a recommendation).
bottom_line.watch_tomorrow: Weekly EIA inventories (Wed), OPEC MOMR follow-through, and Atlantic hurricane tracking.
